entities.name: prakāśya
entities.cite: the illuminated object conforms to the differences of its illuminators

entities.name: prakāśaka
entities.cite: the illuminated object conforms to the differences of its illuminators

entities.name: pratibimba
entities.cite: One face, reflected in water, in oil, in a sword-blade: the pratibimba differs

entities.name: medium
entities.cite: the differences are patently the medium's contribution

entities.name: bheda
entities.cite: the praktbimba differs — dim, dark, elongated — as the medium differs

entities.name: face
entities.cite: One face, reflected in water, in oil, in a sword-blade

entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: the word heard through a child's voice, a shout, a drawl 'conforms' likewise

entities.name: appearance
entities.cite: everyone already distinguishes the thing from its medium-conditioned appearance

axioms.name: anuvartate
axioms.cite: Prakasya anuvartate prakasakanam bhedan: the revealed conforms to its revealers

axioms.name: hetutva
axioms.cite: the differences are patently the medium's contribution, since the face has not changed

axioms.name: aniruddha
axioms.cite: since the face has not changed

axioms.name: anueti
axioms.cite: the pratibimba differs — dim, dark, elongated — as the medium differs

axioms.name: anuvartate
axioms.cite: the word heard through a child's voice, a shout, a drawl 'conforms' likewise — its apparent duration, pitch and colour are the sounds' vicissitudes worn borrowed

denials.name: hetutva
denials.cite: the differences are patently the medium's contribution, since the face has not changed

claims.name: anuvartate
claims.cite: the illuminated object conforms to the differences of its illuminators

claims.name: anueti
claims.cite: that is directly seen in a reflection when the oil, water and the like differ

rejected_readings.label: intrinsic_change
rejected_readings.rendering: The reflected object itself changes in its intrinsic nature when observed through different media.
rejected_readings.why: Treats the face as having properties intrinsic to it that change based on the medium. But the commentary explicitly states 'the face has not changed' and 'the differences are patently the medium's contribution.' This misreading confuses the manifestation (pratibimba) with its ground (face), assigning to the ground properties that belong only to the manifestation.

rejected_readings.label: medium_irrelevant
rejected_readings.rendering: The differences in reflections occur regardless of the medium, showing that the illuminated object is self-determining.
rejected_readings.why: Denies the causal role of the medium in producing the differences. But the verse and commentary are explicit: 'when the oil, water and the like differ' the pratibimba differs. This reading severs the causal relation between medium and differences that the verse centrally asserts.

rejected_readings.label: appearance_is_illusion
rejected_readings.rendering: The medium deceives us about the true nature of the revealed object; only the unchanging ground is real.
rejected_readings.why: While the verse establishes that the face remains unchanged, it does not claim that the appearance is illusory or that perception through media is unreliable. The commentary emphasizes 'this is not speculation but something seen' — treating the appearance as directly observable and informative, not deceptive.

notes: The verse establishes the hetutva (causal relation) between the medium (prakasaka qua distinguishing feature) and the differences (bheda) in how prakasya appears. This is the transfer-doctrine from 1.49 applied to the illumination metaphor: the revealed thing conforms to its revealers, just as a reflection conforms to its medium. The sort taxonomy strains minimally: 'medium' is technically a vehicle or locus rather than a property, but is encoded as property to capture its role in determining appearance. The verse bridges physical reflection (water, oil, metal) and linguistic manifestation (voice of child, shout, drawl), applying the same principle to both.
